title: Recycle bank pressure
summary: Residents want someone to verify whether the outer-ring recycler spike is weather or neglect.

objectives.text: Ask the station guide what corridors are affected.

objectives.prompt: You can push engineering for a public reading or take credits to call it fine.

choices.label: Demand a posted public telemetry readout.
choices.outcome: The spike gets logged; maintenance schedules overtime.

choices.label: Accept a quiet stipend and drop the thread.
choices.outcome: The ducts keep whispering; your account looks healthier.

title: Phantom trunk load
summary: Power dispatch wants a neutral set of eyes on a ghost draw before they escalate.

objectives.text: Check whether General Supply logged any odd high-draw orders.

objectives.prompt: You find a pattern: the draw aligns with after-hours kiosk reboots—or with a masked tap.

choices.label: Report the masked tap hypothesis to dispatch.
choices.outcome: Inspectors crawl the trunk; honest vendors get cleared.

choices.label: Sell the timing pattern to whoever profits from the confusion.
choices.outcome: Someone else pays for certainty; you pay nothing but sleep.

title: Spread on the floor
summary: A clerk asks you to witness a spread correction before signatures land.

objectives.text: Hear what traders are shouting on the promenade connectors.

objectives.prompt: You can sign a fair correction or backdate a favorable tick for a cut.

choices.label: Witness the fair correction as written.
choices.outcome: The board steadies; your name is boring in the best way.

choices.label: Backdate the tick and take the kickback.
choices.outcome: Profits land now; audits may land later.

title: Berthed grudges
summary: Traffic control needs a runner to calm a line of captains arguing over the held berth.

objectives.text: See the docking arm situation at the shipyard.

objectives.prompt: You can relay the honest ETA or auction fake priority slots.

choices.label: Broadcast the honest maintenance ETA.
choices.outcome: Tempers cool; queues reorder without blood.

choices.label: Sell fake priority berthing promises.
choices.outcome: Credits clink in; grudges sharpen on channel nine.

title: Crate jam ethics
summary: Sanitation will clear the recycler queue if someone takes responsibility for mis-filed hazmat.

objectives.text: Walk the affected promenade shoulder and count the worst choke point.

objectives.prompt: You find mis-tagged hazmat mixed with household reclaim.

choices.label: Flag the hazmat and stop the line safely.
choices.outcome: The jam clears slowly but cleanly.

choices.label: Shove the suspect crates through to meet quota.
choices.outcome: The queue moves; the ducts might not forgive you.

title: Sample lane favors
summary: A customs sub-clerk offers to slide a lot forward—for a price or a principle.

objectives.text: Observe the backed-up sample lane near civilian cargo.

objectives.prompt: You can refuse the bribe and insist on order, or grease the lane.

choices.label: Refuse and file a fair-queue notice.
choices.outcome: Inspections stay slow but defensible.

choices.label: Pay the clerk to skip a stranger's lot ahead.
choices.outcome: Someone else's cargo jumps; your conscience invoices you.

title: Surcharge edge
summary: Couriers want a credible read on whether the fuel surcharge rumor is real.

objectives.text: Ask the guide what official channels say about surcharges.

objectives.prompt: You can tell the truth you found—or sell a panic premium.

choices.label: Share the accurate schedule you verified.
choices.outcome: Couriers plan; markets wobble less.

choices.label: Inflate the rumor and sell rush contracts.
choices.outcome: You profit from velocity; someone eats the lie.

title: Permit table tempers
summary: Vendors need a mediator before enforcement arrives.

objectives.text: Reach the congested promenade shoulder.

objectives.prompt: You can help vendors apply for a batch permit—or scatter them with a fake raid whistle.

choices.label: Organize a batch permit application blitz.
choices.outcome: Lines shrink; paperwork explodes politely.

choices.label: Fake a raid whistle and loot abandoned stalls.
choices.outcome: You grab goods; trust on the promenade thins.

title: Tap rumor control
summary: Someone is profiting off a water-window rumor. You can confirm or compound it.

objectives.text: Check whether General Supply is quietly stocking extra jugs.

objectives.prompt: Shelves show a modest stockpile—not a crisis, not nothing.

choices.label: Post a calm clarification in public channels.
choices.outcome: Panic ebbs; hoarders look foolish.

choices.label: Buy out the jugs and resell at panic prices.
choices.outcome: Thirst pays you; anger remembers.

title: Compliant glare
summary: Weld crews want a witness while they swap rigs for the inspection.

objectives.text: Meet the foreman at the shipyard inspection lane.

objectives.prompt: Auditors ask you to sign off on a bay that still flickers noncompliant.

choices.label: Refuse to sign until the bay is fixed.
choices.outcome: Work stops; eyes stay safe.

choices.label: Sign off for a fee and look away.
choices.outcome: Production resumes; retinas might not.

title: Ghost bid chase
summary: Realty IT wants a field check on whether a ghost listing maps to a real parcel.

objectives.text: Review the anomaly at the real estate office.

objectives.prompt: The listing hash matches a sealed dispute parcel.

choices.label: Freeze the listing and escalate to oversight.
choices.outcome: Speculators howl; the record stays honest.

choices.label: Front-run the glitch with a shell bid.
choices.outcome: You might own air; you definitely own risk.

title: Drill escort
summary: Security wants a civilian witness during the dim-cycle drill on one spoke.

objectives.text: Confirm public messaging with the station guide.

objectives.prompt: During the dim cycle, you see a sealed door propped—against procedure.

choices.label: Report the propped door immediately.
choices.outcome: The drill stays by the book.

choices.label: Slip through for a quick shortcut haul.
choices.outcome: You save minutes; you borrow liability.

title: Assayer's line
summary: Miners want someone to read the new grade floor notice beside them.

objectives.text: Hear the argument at Mining Outfitters.

objectives.prompt: You can endorse the stricter floor or forge a sympathetic waiver.

choices.label: Endorse the stricter purity floor publicly.
choices.outcome: Small miners groan; markets gain clarity.

choices.label: Forge a waiver stamp for a desperate seller.
choices.outcome: Credits flow; integrity frays.

title: Cap crowd control
summary: Guides need help redirecting a tour group before the cap alarm trips.

objectives.text: Ask the station guide for alternate routes.

objectives.prompt: You can split the group fairly—or accept a bribe to pack them in anyway.

choices.label: Split the tour across two staggered departures.
choices.outcome: Crowds thin; schedules stretch.

choices.label: Pack them in and take the organizer's bribe.
choices.outcome: Alarms flirt with red; your account smiles.

title: Cold-chain detour
summary: Volunteers suspect selective routing on a med supply line.

objectives.text: Trace manifests at General Supply.

objectives.prompt: Logs show a detour favoring a private clinic sponsor.

choices.label: Expose the detour to public health oversight.
choices.outcome: Routing normalizes; sponsors fume.

choices.label: Sell the detour secret to the sponsor.
choices.outcome: You monetize inequity.

title: Beacon mismatch
summary: A salvage gig looks wrong on paper; you can warn rookies or fleece them.

objectives.text: Read the contract board at the shipyard.

objectives.prompt: Hull registry and beacon hash disagree on the same job.

choices.label: Warn newcomers off the bad gig.
choices.outcome: You lose a finder's fee; you keep a conscience.

choices.label: Sell the bad gig to desperate bidders anyway.
choices.outcome: You profit from someone else's bad math.

title: Dim corridor escort
summary: A nervous clerk asks for an escort through a dimmed industrial sleeve.

objectives.text: Meet the clerk at the promenade transfer hub.

objectives.prompt: Halfway through, someone offers you credits to 'lose' the clerk in the dark.

choices.label: Refuse and finish the escort.
choices.outcome: The clerk tips you what they can; you sleep fine.

choices.label: Take the payment and vanish mid-walk.
choices.outcome: The clerk's fear pays someone's agenda.

title: Terminal triad
summary: Finance wants a witness statement on three kiosk failures in one block.

objectives.text: Check if the kiosk cluster sits near General Supply loading.

objectives.prompt: Timing matches a vendor's unlicensed reboot script.

choices.label: File an honest incident tied to the script.
choices.outcome: Techs patch; blame lands correctly.

choices.label: Extort the vendor for silence.
choices.outcome: Credits flow; audits might follow.

title: Heritage hustle
summary: Heritage week brings opportunity: help the honest booth—or skim the donations.

objectives.text: Visit the heritage loop on the promenade.

objectives.prompt: Donation barrels are unattended for minutes between rotations.

choices.label: Stand watch until the next volunteer arrives.
choices.outcome: Crowds notice; the org remembers.

choices.label: Skim a barrel when no one is looking.
choices.outcome: Charity loses a slice; you gain weight in your pocket.

title: Sensor drill witness
summary: Security needs a civilian signature that calibration was announced properly.

objectives.text: Ask the guide whether cargo advisories were posted.

objectives.prompt: You can sign honestly—or backdate the posting for a fee.

choices.label: Sign only if postings match reality.
choices.outcome: Drill accountability holds.

choices.label: Backdate the posting for credits.
choices.outcome: Paperwork lies; someone profits from deniability.

title: Solvent sleeve
summary: Industrial safety wants a corridor marked or a rumor squashed.

objectives.text: Confirm vent status from the civilian yard side.

objectives.prompt: Sniffer levels are low but real; posting could panic non-essentials.

choices.label: Insist on full public posting and mask stations.
choices.outcome: Crowds grumble; lungs stay safer.

choices.label: Soft-pedal the numbers for station morale.
choices.outcome: Calm stays high; exposure might too.

title: Survey whispers
summary: Miners trade overlapping survey gossip; you can verify or monetize it.

objectives.text: Run a survey check against the gossip.

objectives.prompt: Data shows a borderline overlap—enough to fight over, not enough to prove.

choices.label: Share the ambiguous truth with both parties.
choices.outcome: Tempers cool into paperwork.

choices.label: Auction the tip to the highest bitter bidder.
choices.outcome: You profit from a feud.

title: Lift priority slip
summary: Fork crews offer a priority slip that is not on any official schedule.

objectives.text: See the sub-deck backlog from General Supply's receiving angle.

objectives.prompt: You can refuse the slip and report it, or buy priority for an anonymous crate.

choices.label: Report the unofficial priority slip.
choices.outcome: Queues stay ugly but fair.

choices.label: Buy the slip and move questionable freight.
choices.outcome: Your pallet rises; ethics sink.

title: Drone clutter
summary: Navigation liaison wants a ground truth on whether drones are blinding sensors.

objectives.text: Observe the drone swarm from the shipyard observation gallery.

objectives.prompt: You can certify benign clutter—or sell a scare story to charter brokers.

choices.label: Certify the swarm as maintenance-limited clutter.
choices.outcome: Schedules hold; captains grumble less.

choices.label: Sell an exaggerated hazard bulletin.
choices.outcome: Charters pay risk premiums on fiction.

title: Timestamp tug
summary: Two yards want a third-party nod on whose weld clock is honest.

objectives.text: Review the posted repair tags at the shipyard admin kiosk.

objectives.prompt: Both timestamps are plausible; only one can be billed.

choices.label: Rule for the yard with cleaner logs.
choices.outcome: Billing unfreezes fairly.

choices.label: Rule for whoever pays you more.
choices.outcome: Justice bends; your balance straightens.
